title: Spring Boot Senior Engineer Assessment
description: Senior-level evaluation focusing on advanced Spring Boot concepts: auto-configuration internals, conditional beans, AOP, transaction propagation, security configurations, testing strategies (slice tests), event handling, custom starters, performance tuning, and production-grade considerations.

questions.text: What is the mechanism behind Spring Boot's auto-configuration and how does @ConditionalOnMissingBean work?
options: Auto-configuration uses reflection to scan all classes; @ConditionalOnMissingBean always creates a new bean
Auto-configuration uses spring.factories files; @ConditionalOnMissingBean only creates a bean if no other bean of same type exists in context, allowing custom overrides
Auto-configuration only works with XML configuration
questions.explanation: Spring Boot auto-configuration classes are listed in META-INF/spring.factories or META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports. @ConditionalOnMissingBean allows developers to override defaults by defining their own beans.
hints: Think about how Spring Boot knows which configuration to apply
This annotation lets you replace the default DataSource with your own

questions.text: What is the difference between @ComponentScan and @EntityScan in Spring Boot?
options: @ComponentScan scans Spring-managed beans (@Component, @Service, @Repository); @EntityScan scans JPA entities (@Entity) for EntityManagerFactory creation
@EntityScan is for MongoDB; @ComponentScan is for JPA
questions.explanation: @ComponentScan finds Spring beans. @EntityScan finds JPA entities and is often used when entities are in different packages than the main application class.
hints: One is for dependency injection, one is for ORM mapping
Used together in multi-module projects

questions.text: What is the purpose of @ConditionalOnClass and @ConditionalOnMissingClass annotations?
options: To conditionally create beans based on environment variables
To conditionally create beans based on presence or absence of specific classes in the classpath (e.g., DataSource if HikariCP is present)
questions.explanation: These are core auto-configuration annotations. @ConditionalOnClass ensures configuration only loads when a library (e.g., Kafka, Redis) is on classpath. @ConditionalOnMissingClass does the opposite.
hints: Used extensively in Spring Boot starters
Powers optional dependencies

questions.text: How does Spring Boot determine which DataSource to use when multiple are present (e.g., H2, PostgreSQL, Oracle)?
options: It fails with ambiguous error and requires explicit configuration
It uses @Primary annotation on the preferred DataSource
It uses spring.datasource.url prefix to detect driver (jdbc:h2:, jdbc:postgresql:, etc.) and auto-configures the matching one
It always uses H2 by default
questions.explanation: Spring Boot auto-configuration uses ConnectionFactory to detect database type from JDBC URL. It also checks for specific driver classes to determine the appropriate DataSource implementation.
hints: The JDBC URL contains hints about database type
Each database has unique driver class

questions.text: What is the difference between @Transactional with Propagation.REQUIRED vs Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW?
options: REQUIRED joins existing transaction or creates new; REQUIRES_NEW always suspends current transaction and starts new independent one
REQUIRED is for reads; REQUIRES_NEW is for writes
questions.explanation: REQUIRED (default) participates in existing transaction. REQUIRES_NEW suspends any current transaction, creates new one. Failure in REQUIRES_NEW does not rollback outer transaction.
hints: Think about nested transaction behavior
REQUIRES_NEW creates independent transaction boundary

questions.text: What is the difference between @Transactional on public vs private methods in Spring?
options: @Transactional only works on public methods because Spring AOP uses proxies (JDK dynamic proxies or CGLIB) which cannot intercept private methods
Private methods have higher transaction priority
@Transactional works on private methods in Spring Boot 3.x only
questions.explanation: Spring uses proxy-based AOP. Only public methods are intercepted. Private method calls bypass proxy entirely, making @Transactional ineffective. Use self-injection as workaround.
hints: AOP proxies cannot see private methods
Same limitation applies to @Cacheable, @Async

questions.text: How would you implement a custom Spring Boot Starter?
options: Create a module with auto-configuration class, spring.factories file, and optional @ConfigurationProperties
Cannot create custom starters; only Spring team can
questions.explanation: A custom starter requires: auto-configuration class, configuration properties class (optional), and META-INF/spring.factories (or AutoConfiguration.imports for Spring Boot 2.7+) referencing the auto-configuration.
hints: Follow the pattern of existing starters like spring-boot-starter-web
Need to declare your configuration in spring.factories

questions.text: What is the purpose of @ConfigurationProperties validation using JSR-303 annotations?
options: To validate properties at compile time
To validate property values at application startup (e.g., @NotNull, @Min, @Max on configuration fields) using @Validated
questions.explanation: Add @Validated to @ConfigurationProperties class and use validation annotations. Spring Boot validates properties during binding, failing fast if constraints violated.
hints: Prevents misconfiguration at runtime
Requires spring-boot-starter-validation

questions.text: What is the difference between @MockBean and @MockitoBean (Spring Boot 3.4+)?
options: @MockBean replaces real bean with Mockito mock; @MockitoBean is similar but integrates better with Spring Boot 3.4+ test slice improvements
@MockBean is for integration tests; @MockitoBean is for unit tests
questions.explanation: @MockitoBean (and @MockitoSpyBean) were introduced in Spring Boot 3.4 as improved alternatives to @MockBean, better handling of bean replacement and clean-up between tests.
hints: New in Spring Boot 3.4
Better test isolation than @MockBean

questions.text: What is a 'Slice Test' in Spring Boot (e.g., @DataJpaTest, @WebMvcTest, @JsonTest)?
options: A test that runs only a portion of the application
A test that loads only a specific part of the application context (JPA, MVC, JSON) for faster, focused integration tests
A test that slices database tables
A performance test that measures execution time
questions.explanation: Slice tests load only beans relevant to the test layer. @DataJpaTest loads JPA components, @WebMvcTest loads MVC controllers only, significantly faster than @SpringBootTest.
hints: Reduces test execution time
Each slice focuses on one layer of application

questions.text: How do you test @ControllerAdvice (global exception handler) with @WebMvcTest?
options: @ControllerAdvice cannot be tested with slice tests
questions.explanation: @WebMvcTest only loads controllers by default. Use @Import(YourControllerAdvice.class) to explicitly add global exception handlers to test context.
hints: @ControllerAdvice is not auto-scanned in slice tests
Need to manually import your advice class

questions.text: What is the purpose of @TestConfiguration and how does it differ from @Configuration in tests?
options: @TestConfiguration defines beans only for test context, not loaded in production; can be static inner class or top-level with @Import
questions.explanation: @TestConfiguration beans are only registered in test ApplicationContext. Unlike @Configuration, they are not auto-detected; must be explicitly imported or declared as static inner class.
hints: Test-specific bean definitions
Often used to provide mock dependencies

questions.text: What is the difference between @SpyBean and @MockBean in Spring Boot tests?
options: @MockBean creates complete mock; @SpyBean wraps real bean and allows partial mocking (real method calls unless stubbed)
questions.explanation: @SpyBean delegates to real implementation by default, allowing you to stub only specific methods. @MockBean stubs all methods. Use spies to test real behavior with selective overrides.
hints: One calls real methods by default
Use when you need most real behavior but want to mock one method

questions.text: What is the purpose of 'spring-boot:build-info' Maven/Gradle goal?
options: To generate build-info.properties file with build metadata (version, timestamp, artifact) accessible via /actuator/info
questions.explanation: This goal generates build-info.properties. With Actuator's /info endpoint, you can expose version, build time, and custom properties for monitoring and support.
hints: Works with Actuator info endpoint
Useful for knowing which version is deployed

questions.text: What are the different strategies for external configuration in Spring Boot (order of precedence)?
options: Command line arguments → Environment variables → application-{profile}.properties → application.properties
questions.explanation: Spring Boot external config precedence (highest to lowest): command line args → SPRING_APPLICATION_JSON → env variables → application-{profile}.properties → application.properties → defaults.
hints: Command line overrides everything
Profile-specific files override default

questions.text: How do you implement graceful shutdown in Spring Boot 2.3+?
options: Set server.shutdown=graceful and spring.lifecycle.timeout-per-shutdown-phase=20s; allows in-flight requests to complete before shutdown
questions.explanation: Graceful shutdown waits for active requests to complete within timeout. Configure server.shutdown=graceful and spring.lifecycle.timeout-per-shutdown-phase. Works with Tomcat, Jetty, Undertow.
hints: Prevents request failures during deployment
Requires embedded web server support

questions.text: What is the purpose of @DirtiesContext in Spring Boot tests?
options: To mark ApplicationContext as dirty, forcing context reload before next test (used when tests modify shared state)
questions.explanation: @DirtiesContext tells Spring to close and recreate the context. Use when test modifies context (e.g., changing system properties, mocking static state). Adds overhead, use sparingly.
hints: Prevents test pollution across test classes
Increases test execution time

questions.text: What is the difference between @DynamicPropertySource and Testcontainers?
options: @DynamicPropertySource injects dynamic properties into Spring Environment from containers; Testcontainers manages container lifecycle; they are often used together
Testcontainers is only for unit tests
questions.explanation: Testcontainers starts containers. @DynamicPropertySource reads container info (host, port) and adds to Spring Environment. Used together for real integration tests.
hints: One manages containers, one injects connection properties
Common pattern for database integration tests

questions.text: What is the purpose of @ApplicationTest with @SpringBootTest's webEnvironment = RANDOM_PORT?
options: To run tests on random port to avoid port conflicts in parallel test execution
questions.explanation: RANDOM_PORT starts embedded web server on random available port, accessed via @LocalServerPort. Essential for parallel test execution and CI/CD pipelines.
hints: Prevents 'port already in use' errors
Use @LocalServerPort to get assigned port

questions.text: How does Spring Boot handle circular dependencies between beans?
options: Allows setter injection circular dependencies but not constructor injection; can use @Lazy on one side to break cycle
questions.explanation: Constructor injection circular dependencies cause BeanCurrentlyInCreationException. Use setter/field injection or @Lazy on one dependency to break the cycle.
hints: Constructor injection is safer but stricter
@Lazy delays bean initialization

questions.text: What is the purpose of 'spring-boot-starter-parent' in Maven?
options: To provide default dependency versions, plugin configurations, and Java version (17/21) via parent POM inheritance
questions.explanation: Starter parent provides dependency management (Bill of Materials), plugin management (spring-boot-maven-plugin), and default Java version. Simplifies POM files significantly.
hints: Parent POM pattern in Maven
Manages versions of all Spring dependencies

questions.text: What is the difference between @SpringBootApplication and @EnableAutoConfiguration?
questions.explanation: @SpringBootApplication is convenience annotation combining three essential annotations. Use @EnableAutoConfiguration when you need finer control over component scanning.
hints: One is three annotations in one
Most applications use the combined annotation

questions.text: How do you exclude a specific auto-configuration class in Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: Exclude auto-configuration using @SpringBootApplication's exclude attribute or spring.autoconfigure.exclude property. Useful when you want custom configuration for specific component.
hints: Prevents unwanted auto-configuration
Two different ways (annotation and property)

questions.text: What is the purpose of @EventListener's condition attribute (SpEL)?
options: To conditionally execute event handler based on event properties (e.g., @EventListener(condition = "#event.userId == 'admin'")
questions.explanation: SpEL condition allows filtering events before handler execution. Access event properties via #event variable. Useful for selective processing.
hints: Spring Expression Language powers this
Evaluated before method execution

questions.text: What is the difference between @Async with proxy mode and @Async with AspectJ mode?
options: Proxy mode (default) uses Spring AOP (only public methods, external calls); AspectJ mode uses compile-time/load-time weaving (can intercept private and internal calls)
Proxy mode is only for @Component classes
questions.explanation: Proxy mode limitations: only public methods, calls within same class bypass proxy. AspectJ mode (requires @EnableAsync(mode = AdviceMode.ASPECTJ)) weaves bytecode, enabling any method.
hints: Proxy mode has limitations with self-invocation
AspectJ requires compilation weaving or load-time weaving
